title: Method of etching crystalline material
abstract: A method of etching crystalline diamond is disclosed. The method comprises depositing a catalytic metal (20) on a body of crystalline diamond (4), and causing the catalytic metal to react with the diamond to provide a metal carbide. A gas mixture (26) is reacted with carbon from the metal carbide to provide a gaseous material which includes carbon, and a eutectic regulating gas is provided for increasing the rate of provision of the carbon-containing gaseous material.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method of etching crystalline material, the method comprising: depositing a first material on a body of a second material, wherein the first material is a catalytic material and the second material is a crystalline material including diamond; causing the first material to react with the second material to provide a third material, wherein the third material includes carbon from said diamond; causing a fourth material to react with said carbon from said third material to provide a fifth material, wherein the fifth material is a gaseous material and includes said carbon; and providing a sixth material for increasing a rate of provision of said fifth material. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein said sixth material is adapted to regulate a eutectic state of said third material at least at a surface of said third material. 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein said sixth material is adapted to impede fragmentation of said first material. 4. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said sixth material comprises ammonia. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the fourth material includes a seventh material adapted to react with the third material to release said carbon from said third material, and an eighth material adapted to react with the released carbon. 6. A method according to claim 5, wherein the seventh material includes an oxidising agent. 7. A method according to claim 6, wherein the seventh material includes oxygen. 8. A method according to any one of claims 5 to 7, wherein the eighth material includes a reducing agent. 9. A method according to claim 8, wherein the eighth material includes hydrogen. 10. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising providing a ninth material adapted to inhibit fragmentation of said first material. 11. A method according to claim 10, wherein said ninth material is deposited on said second material with said first material. 12. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising providing a tenth material adapted to inhibit the reaction of said first material with said second material. 13. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the third material is a carbide. 14. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising controlling the temperature of the body of second material. 15. A method according to claim 14, wherein the temperature of the body of second material is controlled to maintain a temperature of a chemical reaction between the first and second materials within predetermined limits. 16. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the step of depositing the first material on the body of second material is carried out by means of at least one lithographic step. 17. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising applying a force to at least part of said first material to enhance penetration of said second material by said first material. 18. A method according to claim 17, wherein the force is applied electrostatically and/or magnetically. 19. A method of etching crystalline material, the method comprising: depositing a first material on a body of a second material, wherein the first material is a catalytic material and the second material is a crystalline material including diamond; 

causing the first material to react with the second material to provide a third material, wherein the third material includes carbon from said diamond; causing a fourth material to react with said carbon from said third material to provide a fifth material, wherein the fifth material is a gaseous material and includes said carbon; and providing a ninth material for inhibiting fragmentation of said first material. 20. A method according to claim 19, wherein said ninth material is deposited on said second material with said first material. 21. A method according to claim 19 or 20, wherein said ninth material comprises copper. 22. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 21, further comprising providing a sixth material for increasing a rate of provision of said fifth material. 23. A method according to claim 22, wherein said sixth material is adapted to regulate a eutectic state of said third material at least at a surface of said third material. 24. A method according to claim 22 or 23, wherein said sixth material is adapted to impede fragmentation of said first material. 25. A method according to any one of claims 22 to 24, wherein said sixth material comprises ammonia. 26. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 25, wherein the fourth material includes a seventh material adapted to react with the third material to release carbon from said third material, and an eighth material adapted to react with the released carbon. 27. A method according to claim 26, wherein the seventh material includes an oxidising agent. 28. A method according to claim 27, wherein the seventh material includes oxygen. 29. A method according to any one of claims 26 to 28, wherein the eighth material includes a reducing agent. 

 -21- 30. A method according to claim 29, wherein the eighth material includes hydrogen. 31. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 30, further comprising providing a tenth material adapted to inhibit the reaction of said first material with said second material. 32. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 31, wherein the third material is a carbide. 33. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 32, further comprising controlling the temperature of the body of second material. 34. A method according to claim 33, wherein the temperature of the body of second material is controlled to maintain a temperature of a chemical reaction between the first and second materials within predetermined limits. 35. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 34, wherein the step of depositing the first material on the body of second material is carried out by means of at least one lithographic step. 36. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 35, further comprising applying a force to at least part of said first material to enhance penetration of said second material by said first material. 37. A method according to claim 36, wherein the force is applied electrostatically and/or magnetically. 38. A method of etching a crystalline material, the method comprising: depositing at least one catalytic material on a body of crystalline material including diamond; causing the catalytic material to react with said crystalline material at a first temperature, wherein said catalytic material preferentially etches said crystalline material in a first crystallographic direction; and causing said catalytic material to react with said crystalline material at a second temperature, different from said first temperature, such that said catalytic material preferentially etches said crystalline material in a second crystallographic direction, different from said first crystallographic direction. 

 -22- 39. A method according to claim 38, wherein the catalytic material is at least one metal capable of forming an unstable compound comprising of reactive material and the target etch material at elevated temperatures. 40. A method according to claim 38 or 39, further comprising applying a force to at least part of said catalytic material to enhance penetration of said crystalline material by said catalytic material. 41. A method according to claim 40, wherein the force is applied electrostatically and/or magnetically.
